pr.title: some grgreatbay data matching + attribute labelling
pr.author: MarioLover63

summary: Matched and clarified Great Bay stage data in src/melee/gr/grgreatbay.c. The PR renamed grGb_StageAttr fields from raw offset names to behavior-oriented names for moon timing, floating floor response, Kame/turtle movement, direction probabilities, item probability, and item weights. It also added inline labels for the StageCallbacks entries, replaced extern declarations for grGb_803B81C8 and grGb_803B81D4 with concrete const data definitions, and made small local-variable/PAD_STACK adjustments needed for matching.
what_changed: Annotated grGb_803E3E84 StageCallbacks entries with object-role comments: turtle, main stage, background, foreground water, giant 1-4, moon, and tingle.

Renamed grGb_StageAttr fields from offset placeholders such as x0, x4, x68, x70, and x7C to semantic attribute names including moon_fall_wait_a/b, floatfloor_* parameters, kame_* parameters, kame_dir_prob, kame_item_prob, and items.

Updated all uses of grGb_804D69E0.x0 stage attributes to the new semantic names in Great Bay turtle/Kame logic, moon timing logic, floating floor damage response, direction randomization, and item spawning.
Renamed a local HSD_JObj* in grGreatBay_801F499C from jobj2 to trans_jobj, making its role as the transform joint clearer while preserving assert line numbers.
Defined const Vec3 grGb_803B81C8 = { 0.99f, 35.72f, 0.0f } and const SpawnItem grGb_803B81D4 in-place instead of leaving them as extern declarations, contributing to data matching.
Adjusted stack padding in at least grGreatBay_801F60C4 and grGreatBay_801F63F4, including changing PAD_STACK values and adding a local s32 padding in grGreatBay_801F63F4.
smart_moves: Kept struct offsets and member types intact while renaming fields, preserving binary layout and making attribute use self-documenting.
Grouped attribute names by subsystem behavior: moon timing, floating-floor landing/slant/slide/down response, Kame/turtle position/timing/scale/probabilities, and item spawn data.
Used field names that encode mathematical role, such as *_mul, *_add, *_limit, *_rate, and *_reb_rate, which makes formulas readable without changing logic.
Labelled callback table rows rather than renaming unknown callback functions, adding useful stage-object context without overclaiming function identities.
Matched data by replacing extern declarations with concrete const initializers for Vec3 and SpawnItem while retaining explicit field/comment offsets in the SpawnItem initializer.
Made matching-sensitive stack changes locally and minimally, suggesting the author corrected stack layout without broad refactors.
decomp_lessons: For stage attribute structs, semantic field names can often be inferred from use sites: random wait ranges, scale factors, motion offsets, limits, and probability tables reveal their purpose.
Preserve offset comments when renaming anonymous struct fields; this keeps the struct useful for matching and for cross-checking against data blobs.
Callback arrays benefit from object-role comments when function names are still address-based; comments make the stage object ordering searchable and reduce future reverse-engineering friction.
When decompiling weighted random selection, naming arrays as probabilities or weighted items clarifies the algorithm while leaving the exact iteration/order intact.
Data definitions can be introduced directly in the C file when known constants are available, but matching may require careful stack-padding and local-variable adjustments nearby.

For formulas driven by stage attributes, names like landing_rate, slant_mul, slant_add, slant_limit, slide_rate, and down_limit are more reusable than raw xNN offsets and make future behavior validation easier.
naming_conventions: Great Bay stage attribute typedef retained the stage prefix style: grGb_StageAttr.
Stage-global symbols retained existing grGb_ address-style names such as grGb_803E3E84, grGb_803E3F6C, grGb_803B81C8, grGb_803B81D4, and grGb_804D69E0.
Attribute fields use lower_snake_case semantic names.
Paired min/max or range endpoints were named with _a and _b suffixes where exact min/max semantics were not asserted, e.g. moon_fall_wait_a/b, kame_wait_frame_a/b, and kame_rebirth_frame_a/b.
Floating-floor attributes use the floatfloor_ prefix and role suffixes such as _mul, _add, _limit, _rate, _reb_rate, _up_rate, and _down_rate.
Kame/turtle attributes use the kame_ prefix for timing, base position, offsets, scale, direction probability, item probability, and item list fields.
The item table was renamed from x7C to items, with per-entry fields kind and weight already present or preserved.
A transform joint local was named trans_jobj to distinguish it from the primary jobj and jobj3.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Changed PAD_STACK(12) to PAD_STACK(4) in grGreatBay_801F60C4 after semantic field renames, indicating stack-size tuning for match.
In grGreatBay_801F63F4, added an explicit s32 padding local and changed PAD_STACK(8) to PAD_STACK(4), a common tactic to control stack layout while keeping source readable.
Concrete const data definitions for grGb_803B81C8 and grGb_803B81D4 replaced externs, allowing previously external data to be matched in this translation unit.
Kept assert line numbers stable while renaming locals, e.g. HSD_ASSERT(529, jobj) and HSD_ASSERT(530, trans_jobj).
Preserved odd indexing/order in probability calculation, such as summing kame_dir_prob[0], [1], [3], [2] and subtracting in the observed order, rather than simplifying it and risking mismatch.
Preserved offset-based loop structure for item weights using offset / 4, rather than rewriting as a direct array loop everywhere, likely to maintain matching codegen.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that move grGb_StageAttr to a header or further rename Great Bay Ground gv fields.
Search for other stage files using *_StageAttr offset placeholders that can be labelled from behavior formulas.
Check whether grGb_803B81D4 SpawnItem field comments were later replaced by designated initializers or named struct fields.
Look for follow-up work on grGreatBay_801F499C and grGreatBay_801F63F4 to see if kame/turtle state variables xC4, xC5, xC6, xC8, and xCC were named.
Search for similar floating-floor attribute naming in other stages to standardize floatfloor_ prefixes and rate/rebound terminology.
